title: carya ovata f. halesii
intro: Carya ovata f. halesii, also known as the Shagbark Hickory, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive shaggy bark and edible nuts. The medicinal properties of Carya ovata f. halesii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood sugar levels, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Carya ovata f. halesii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its nuts can be used as a food source, providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
